texts.full_text: his oath. The colony fell under corrupt criminal control. Whatever was ‘out there’, started to seem much better. People started to leave. At the end of the game, most of the colony escaped, and the last scene was between the Dimber Damber’s lieutenant and the leader of the bolters, the mutated men and women who have adapted to the environment and became ‘something else’. Because players always get to establish what the scene is about before it begins, there was always a heavy amount of discussion. We usually dismissed the first idea that came to the table. The instinct was to dig deeper, find something a little more interesting. Whereas Fiasco games are ‘Yes, and’, this was more ‘No, but how about’. And then ‘no’ was usually targeted at self. As in, no, I can think of something better. Which is actually something I prefer I think. The gonzo aspect was almost non-existent. Instead we had hard-hitting drama in a tough environment. You can usually tell a good game apart in the convention by how much cheering and laughter there is. Ours must have looked like the dullest of them all. But we, the players, knew better. We were experiencing a gritty story and it had us captivated. Every scene usually ended up as a confrontation in dialogue between two characters. It would reach a certain point when we didn’t know which character should give in. We then rolled for Uncertainty (a game mechanic meant to move the game along and suggest options), and proceeded accordingly. If at any point two of the dice return with the same result, an event happens. Strangely enough, every time it happened in our game, the event was ridiculously apt for the current situation. texts.full_text: ram: Fresh Air on WPRL 'Alien: Covenant' Mixes Blood And Guts With Existential Questions By David Edelstein • May 19, 2017 Related Program: Fresh Air on WPRL TweetShareGoogle+Email Listen Listening... / Originally published on May 23, 2017 10:38 am Copyright 2017 Fresh Air. To see more, visit Fresh Air. DAVID BIANCULLI, HOST: This is FRESH AIR. The alien film cycle began in 1979 with Ridley's "Alien." It ended in 1997 with "Alien: Resurrection." Now it begins again with "Alien: Covenant," which opens on Friday. It's the first of three planned films, including one that will serve as a prequel to the original. Ridley Scott directed both "Alien" and this new film, which is set 10 years before the events in the original movie. The story begins with a spaceship called Covenant leaving Earth on its way to colonize a distant planet when it is suddenly struck by a nearby supernova explosion. The crew and passengers are all humanoid androids who have been programmed to follow orders without question, so they don't react as humans would in an emergency. The first two hours of "Alien: Covenant" are actually the most interesting part of the film because it's set on a starship that is under attack by an alien virus. The crew and passengers must try to survive while trying to find out what happened and who caused it all, which adds some tension to the plot. However, as the story progresses, the movie becomes more like a standard sci-fi action film with plenty of explosions and battles between humans and aliens. It's not that bad, but it doesn't really add anything new or exciting to the franchise either. Overall, "Alien:

No. 6,381,112 to DiSalvo, which is incorporated by reference herein, a GFCI is provided with a lockout feature which prevents the GFCI from being reset if the solenoid fails to operate or if an open neutral condition exists. However, having a permanent lockout, which prevents the GF...CI device's functionality in case of failure can lead to safety issues and should be carefully evaluated. It is recommended that such GFCIs include a temporary shutdown feature instead of permanently locking out their operation if there are any failures or conditions detected. texts.full_text: the most common causes of severe long-term pain and physical disability affecting hundreds of millions of people worldwide \[[@b1]\]. Thus, bone, cartilage, tendon, and ligament injuries have serious socioeconomic consequences; for example, osteoarthritis affects nearly 27 million Americans or 12.1% of the adult population of the united States with a total annual cost of about \$89.1 billion \[[@B2]\]. Besides osteoarthritis, also bone fracture care in osteoporotic patients has a high incidence with an annual cost of about \$17 billion \[[@B3]\]. Similarly, ligamentous and tendinous injUries are very common with an annual incidence estimated at about 1 per 1000 people \[[@B4], [@B5]\]. However, the bulk of thescosts is not borne by patients themselves but rather by public health systems. The socioeconomic burden associated with these injuries includes direct medical expenses such as hospitalization, surgery, and rehabilitation, as well as indirect costs like lost productivity due to work absence or disability. Moreover, the psychological impact on individuals suffering from chronic pain and mobility limitations cannot be overlooked. It significantly affects their quality of life, leading to depression, anxiety, and social isolation in many cases \[[@B6]\]. To address these challenges, extensive research has been devoted to developing effective strategies for tissue repair and regeneration. This includes advancements in biomaterials science, cell therapy, growth factor delivery systems, and gene therapies aimed at enhancing the body's natural healing processes or replacing damaged tissues entirely \[[@B7]\]. texts.full_text: 9 glycoprotein genes found in wild-type and vaccine strains of varicella-zoster virus. The complete DNA sequences of wild-type and vaccine strains of varicella-zoster virus have been published and listed in GenBank. In this comparative genomic analysis, the sequences of the 9 glycoprotein open reading frames (ORFs) were compared. The results revealed that all nine ORF amino acid sequence identities ranged from approximately equal to or greater than wild type for ORF2 through -80% identity with vaccine strain pOka/pELm in six of the 9 glycoprotein genes, including gE and its family members. The nucleotide sequences showed less variation between strains; however there was significant variability within each gene's codon usage patterns which may contribute to differences seen at protein level when comparing wild type versus vaccinal

Such recordings are called electrograms (EGs) because they register voltage changes between/among points on/in/around cardiac tissue; however when both electrodes contact body surfaces other than those of organs/tissues such as heart or brain, these voltages represent what is referred to conventionally--but not accurately since it does _not_ mean that there are multiple hearts generating electrical activity within one individual organism!--as electrocardiograms (ECGs). This terminology arises from the fact most commonly used recording electrodes contact skin overlying skeletal muscle. Thus EGs can be obtained using any combination of points where an electrode might make physical or electronic [wireless] connection with tissue: endocardial/epicardium, epicadium/mesocardi/peridarium; but also intracellular/extracelluar. It should therefore now become apparent that there are two aspects involved here which need consideration before deciding how many leads will suffice for obtaining all possible information about drug effects on cardiac electrophysiology (EP) from ECGs: First, what properties of EP can be assessed by analyzing EG/ECG waveforms? texts.full_text: ...absolutely fascinating. I've always been a history buff, and the museum did not disappoint. The exhibits were so well curated, and I particularly enjoyed the sections on Indigenous history and the environmental history of Australia. It was a great way to learn more about the country's past and its diverse cultures. Mum and I also spent a lot of time just walking around the city, taking in the architecture and the beautiful parks. The cold weather was a nice change from the usual heat, and it made the walks even more enjoyable. We also indulged in some great food, and I was thrilled to find a café that made the most delicious hot chocolate I've had in a long time. As for the maternity gear, I've never been a fan of pink, so the purple outfits Mum found for me were a real treat. I've been feeling a lot more comfortable, and the baby seems to be happy too, judging by the constant kicking. I've been trying to get as much rest as I can, knowing that the days of peaceful sleep are numbered. Booking the hospital bed has made everything feel more real. I've been a mix of emotions—excitement, nervousness, and a bit of anxiety. But I know that I've got a great support system, and that's been a real comfort. Next on the list of 'C' places to visit is maybe the Coast, though I'm not sure which one yet. Any suggestions are welcome! Until the next rant, [Your Name] P.S. I'm also reading a fantastic book on the history of feminism, which I'll write about next time. Stay tuned! P.P.S. Any tips on how to deal with the final stretch of pregnancy are also very welcome. texts.full_text: is Rule: iptables -I INPUT -i eth1.10 -d ! 192.168.10.0/28 -j DROP I was wondering if it was possible to somehow enforce the Rule on an Interface level, so that the interface would only respond on an ICMP Request directly to the eth1.10 (192.168.10.1/28) interface. Without this Rule, eth1 (192.168.1.1/24) is also pingable via eth1.10, which is a security flaw in my eyes. Edit for Clarifications: The Router is routing between these two subnets. I split the four Ports up on the back of the Router so i can plug my Server into Port 4 of the Router. I then associated Port 4 with eth1.10, eth1 is connected to the other 3 Ports, which are for LAN use. I want to completely isolate the network 192.168.1.0 from 192.168.10.0... # SwitchPort Setup script swconfig dev switch0 set enable_vlan 1 swconfig dev switch0 vlan 1 set ports "2 3 4 6" swconfig dev switch0 vlan 10 set ports "1 6t" swconfig dev switch0 set apply vconfig add eth1 10 ifconfig eth1.10 192.168.10.1 netmask 255.255.255.240 A: (I admit I don't understand all the switch settings but that's not relevant to your specific question about blocking ping on an interface level.) To ensure that only ICMP requests directly addressed to `eth1.10` (i.e., 192.168.10.1) are responded to, while dropping any other ICMP traffic destined for the subnet but not specifically to this IP, you can refine your firewall rules as follows: ```sh # Drop all incoming ICMP packets on eth1.10 except those addressed directly to its IP (192.168.10.1) iptables -I INPUT -i eth1.10 -p icmp --icmp-type echo-request ! --destination 192.168.10.1 -j DROP ``` This rule specifically drops ICMP Echo Request packets (`ping`) that are not destined for `192.168.10.1` but arrive on the `eth1.10` interface. Additionally, to ensure that the router only responds to pings directed at its own IP and does not forward or respond to any other ICMP traffic between subnets: ```sh # Drop all incoming ICMP packets from eth1 (LAN) destined for 192.168.10.0/28 subnet iptab

texts.full_text: of social skill deficits in children with ADHD, there is no consensus regarding a social cognitive profile and whether these skills predict behavior. Therefore, a comprehensive battery was used to investigate the relationship between social cognition and behavioral functioning. Children ages 7 to 13 with ADHD ( n = 25) and controls ( n = 25) completed tests assessing social cognitive domains (affect recognition and theunderstanding of others' beliefs), as well as measures evaluating externalizing, internalzing, and peer relation problems over a six-month period. Results indicated that children with ADHD performed significantly worse than control participants on all aspects except for false belief tasks at Time 1 but these group differences were no longer significant after controlling age or IQ in the analyses (p > .05). Furthermore social cognitive measures predicted unique variance of later behavioral problems even when accounting initial behavior levels and medication status. Specifically, affect recognition significantly contributed to predicting externalizing behaviors over time whereas understanding others' beliefs was a predictor for internalzing symptoms as well peer relation difficulties ahead six months suggesting that these two domains represent different aspects within the broad construct social cognition in ADHD children

texts.full_text: may also include other fodder material including grains and the like. Forage is often transported to cattle or other animals through the use of a forage box supported on a trailer or truck for ready transport. The forage box may receive forage, for example, directly from a tractor-mounted shredder during a harvesting process, traveling next to the tractor during that harvesting. The forage boxes may then be transported to a location removed from the harvesting site where the forage will be stored or dispensed to the animals. Typically forage boxes provide for a mechanism for ejecting forage from the forage box after the forage box has been filled. This mechanism is often in the form of an apron conveyor consisting of chains that pass along tines at each end and also across various transverse cross-members supporting some portion, or all portions thereof. The present invention relates to a novel arrangement whereby such ejecting mechanisms may be more easily used by having control switches located for easy access while sitting inside said trai

texts.full_text: questions about accountability and security vulnerabilities in the automotive supply chain. Authorities are now investigating whether Ford’s production processes or logistics systems were exploited by cartel operatives to embed drugs during manufacturing or assembly. The Sinaloa Cartel’s use of these vehicles underscores a shift in smuggling tactics, as traditional cross-border routes become more heavily monitored. By embedding drugs into components or vehicle cavities during production, cartel members can bypass standard inspections at border crossings. In this case, the marijuana was reportedly hidden in the cars’ undercarriages or within modified body panels, invisible to routine checks until the vehicles reached dealerships or private buyers. Ford has issued a statement condemning the use of its vehicles for illegal activities and confirming that it is cooperating fully with law enforcement. The company has also launched an internal investigation to identify how the smuggling occurred and to tighten security at its Hermosillo plant. Meanwhile, U.S. officials are pressuring Mexican authorities to enhance oversight of automotive manufacturing facilities, which could become fronts in the ongoing battle against drug trafficking. texts.full_text: p or journey can provide, you will not go wrong when you opt to hire a double decker bus to treat your family, colleagues or friends on a day out somewhere, especially if you want to entertain a group that is simply too large for any form of the usual transport one normally associates with group outings. Of course it does not make financial sense to hire a double decker bus for smaller groups who can easily be transported to and from any venue, unless the host or hostess has a budget that allows them to do this. Because booking a whole bus for a private group is such an unusual event, it promises to not only entertain them for the day but to also let them leave with special memories they can cherish for many years after the event.
